questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.prompt: In the context of international security operations, 'Operation HAECHI-VI' is best described as:

options.text: A maritime security initiative by the European Union Naval Force in the Gulf of Aden.
A global operation coordinated by Interpol to combat transnational cyber-enabled financial crimes.
A drone-based offensive strategy used to target strategic bomber aircraft at air bases.
A humanitarian assistance and disaster relief exercise conducted between India and the US.

questions.explanation: Operation HAECHI-VI is the sixth iteration of a series of global operations led by Interpol to combat various forms of transnational cyber-enabled financial crimes. It is financially supported by the Republic of Korea, and India's CBI recently arrested eight operatives under this framework.
questions.takeaway: Operation HAECHI is a recurring Interpol-led global effort specifically targeting financial cybercrimes.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: Operation HAECHI-VI: CBI has arrested eight key operatives of transnational cybercrimes in Interpol Operation HAECHI-VI. Financially supported by the Republic of Korea. It is the sixth iteration of a series of global operations to combat various forms of transnational cyber-enabled financial crimes.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.prompt: The term 'Operation Spider Web', recently seen in the news, refers to:

options.text: A coordinated crackdown on Darknet drug trafficking by the Narcotics Control Bureau.
An offensive involving first-person view (FPV) drones against strategic air bases.
A deep-sea cable protection mission in the Indian Ocean Region.
A multinational effort to track illegal migration through the Mediterranean.

questions.explanation: Operation Spider Web was conducted by Ukraine’s SBU (Security Service). It involved drone attacks using first-person view (FPV) drones on strategic bomber aircraft at Russian air bases, such as the Belaya air base in Siberia.
questions.takeaway: Operation Spider Web represents a shift toward using low-cost FPV drones for high-value strategic military targets in modern warfare.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: Operation Spider Web: Conducted by Ukraine’s SBU (Security Service/Agency). It is a drone attack (using first-person view (FPV) drones) on strategic bomber aircrafts at Russian air bases.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.prompt:
Match the following security operations/exercises with their respective partner entities or regions:

1. Operation ATALANTA : European Union
2. Nomadic Elephant : Mongolia
3. Bold Kurukshetra : Singapore
4. Mission Sankalp : Karregutta Hills

How many of the above pairs are correctly matched?
questions.explanation: All pairs are correctly matched. Operation ATALANTA is the European Union Naval Force (EUNAVFOR) operation. Nomadic Elephant is a joint military exercise with Mongolia. Bold Kurukshetra is a joint military exercise with Singapore. Mission Sankalp is an anti-Naxal mission launched on the Karregutta hills along the Chhattisgarh-Telangana border.
questions.takeaway: Familiarity with the specific partner nations and geographic focus of security operations is vital for matching-type questions.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: Operation ATALANTA: European Union Naval Force (EUNAVFOR)... Nomadic Elephant: 17th edition of India- Mongolia... Exercise Bold Kurukshetra: 14th Edition of India-Singapore... Mission Sankalp: launched on sprawling hills of Karregutta.
